identity.title: Light-Driven Matter, Interfaces, and Future Technologies
identity.introduction: Phoenix Lab takes an interdisciplinary approach to exploring optical technologies as a generative medium for reimagining matter, interfaces, energy, and future interactive systems. At the intersection of Optical Technologies and Human-Computer Interaction, we create programmable materials, physical interfaces, robotic and energy systems, and AI-enabled experiences that reshape how people interact with and through the physical world.
identity.leaderLine: Phoenix Lab is led by Prof. Yuhua Jin at The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen.

news.text: PHOENIX Lab website is under construction.

projects.title: Photo-Chromeleon
projects.punchline: Recolor physical objects like a chameleon using light.
projects.description: A reprogrammable color-changing material system that allows physical objects to be digitally recolored after fabrication. By projecting light onto coated objects, users can write, erase, and update color patterns on real-world surfaces.

projects.title: BlimpMate
projects.punchline: A quiet flying display for soft, situated interaction.
projects.description: A quiet, long-endurance flying display that explores soft, situated, and embodied interaction in physical space.

projects.title: Laser-Powered Interaction
projects.punchline: Battery-free tactile devices powered and controlled by light.
projects.description: Wireless interactive devices powered and controlled by light, enabling battery-free tactile and physical interfaces.

publications.title: [TEST] Light-Programmable Interfaces for Everyday Objects
publications.abstract: [TEST] This placeholder abstract describes a speculative system for reconfiguring the visual and tactile behavior of everyday objects through projected light. It is included only for layout testing.

publications.title: [TEST] Optical Haptics for Battery-Free Tangible Controls
publications.abstract: [TEST] We present a mock research prototype that explores light-powered tangible controls for interactive environments. The entry tests multi-line titles, venue metadata, DOI display, and abstract expansion.

publications.title: [TEST] AI-Assisted Design of Responsive Material Samples
publications.abstract: [TEST] This fabricated entry evaluates how publication cards behave with a longer abstract. It references AI-assisted ideation, fabrication planning, optical sensing, and human-material interaction as sample research themes.

publications.title: [TEST] Situated Flying Displays for Soft Spatial Interaction
publications.abstract: [TEST] This placeholder publication is used to inspect spacing between entries, link wrapping, and the visual rhythm of the publication list when several items are present.

teaching.description: Classes taught by the lab lead will be introduced here.

join.title: Join PHOENIX Lab
join.intro: PHOENIX Lab welcomes curious, creative, and hands-on researchers who are excited about light-driven matter, interfaces, energy systems, and future interactive technologies. We welcome people from HCI, computer science, optical engineering, electronic engineering, materials science, mechanical engineering, robotics, design, and related fields.

sections.title: Postdoctoral Researchers
sections.body: We are recruiting postdoctoral researchers interested in interdisciplinary research related to light-driven materials, programmable matter, optical systems, physical interfaces, robotic systems, energy systems, fabrication, AI-enabled interactive systems, and human-computer interaction.

sections.title: PhD and MPhil Students
sections.body: We welcome prospective PhD and MPhil students interested in creating future physical and interactive technologies at the intersection of optical technologies and human-computer interaction.

sections.title: Research Assistants & Interns
sections.body: We welcome research assistants, visiting students, undergraduate interns, and master鈥檚 interns interested in hands-on interdisciplinary research across experiments, prototypes, hardware, software, AI-enabled interaction, user studies, and visual documentation.

sections.title: Wildcard
sections.body: If your background is not listed but you see a strong and creative connection with PHOENIX Lab, please feel free to reach out.

sections.title: How to Apply
sections.body: Interested applicants may email Prof. Yuhua Jin with a brief introduction, CV, representative publications or portfolios, transcripts if applicable, and a short explanation of why they are interested in PHOENIX Lab.

addressLines: The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen
contact.note: For prospective students, postdoctoral researchers, research assistants, and interns, please visit the Join Us page before contacting us.
